content.full_text:
September 4, 1992

HAND DELIVERED

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Scott:

Pursuant to our meeting this morning, this is a request for written advice pursuant to Gov't Code § 83114(b).

This firm is counsel to the Committee on Jobs ("JOBS"), a non-profit corporation established to promote a healthy business climate in the City of San Francisco. JOBS is a membership organization consisting of major corporations in the San Francisco Bay Area. JOBS does not sponsor or make contributions to any political committees. As part of its overall program, JOBS publishes a quarterly newsletter entitled "CityScope", which deals with issues related to the economic vitality of San Francisco.

This Fall, in its fourth edition of "CityScope", JOBS intends to run election-related material dealing with the positions of various persons who are candidates for public office. The articles will express agreement or disagreement with those positions. The newsletter will be published in the same format as the previous three issues and will be of approximately the same length. Distribution will be to JOBS members, who in turn, will distribute the newsletter to their employees. "CityScope" will also be distributed to those who request a copy of the publication. Although a limited number of copies of two of the previous three issues were sent to persons who did not request the publication, the Fall edition will be restricted to persons who are employees of JOBS members or who request a copy.

Please confirm your advice that, under the facts described above, the cost of publishing the Fall edition of "CityScope" is

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
September 4, 1992
Page 2

not a "contribution", an "expenditure" or an "independent expenditure" as those terms are defined in Gov't Code §§ 82015, 82025 and 82031 and the regulations promulgated thereunder by your Commission (2 Cal.Code Regs. §§ 18215 and 18225).

We appreciate your cooperation and assistance on this matter.

Very truly yours,

Frederick K. Lowell

cc: Mr. Wayne Ordos
    Ms. Carla Wardlow
    Ms. K. E. Donovan
